title: Cicerbita chitralensis
intro: Cicerbita chitralensis, also known as Chitral dandelion or Chitral lettuce, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Cicerbita chitralensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to promote digestive health and boost the immune system.
taxonomy: Cicerbita chitralensis belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is a species of flowering plant. The genus Cicerbita is a group of plants within the family Asteraceae. Cicerbita chitralensis is a rare and endangered species native to the Chitral District in Pakistan. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, further categorizing its unique characteristics.
